helpful: Full provisioned petition text (29 pp., untruncated) — carried the whole qualitative analysis
statpack per-fee-class detail (paid ~5.4% vs IFP ~1.1% Term 2025) — the right anchor for a paid petition
statpack relist-count cut (0-relist 0.8%) as the reference class for a first-distribution petition
gaps: fedcourts query has no free-text/subject filter, so arbitration/evident-partiality priors could not be targeted (SCOTUS rows carry no --topic); a subject or QP-keyword filter would make corpus priors useful for cert cells
statpack.md in this build has no 'Segment base rate by salience band' table although the prompt points to it
a docket-level signal for 'respondent waived response / CFR issued' in the statpack cuts would quantify the strongest deny signal in this case
notes: Statpack alone was sufficient for calibration; the one corpus query returned only generic recent priors and did not move the estimate.
